content.claim: A 2013 centralization of counseling services under state management (KLIK) was followed by a documented shift in time allocation toward assessment/paperwork and away from therapeutic service provision, representing an organizational-design driver of capacity misallocation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: The causal relationship between 2013 centralization and subsequent workload reallocation is based on historical narrative rather than quantified time-allocation data before and after the reform
No independent time-use audit of the centralized service was found
The specific mechanism by which centralization created this reallocation (e.g., through changed management directives vs. external demand pressure) is not detailed
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
